description:
Add optional target_branch: Option<String> field to the Task struct. When set on a single task, overrides the repo-level target_branch for that task's bl review.

WHY: git-flow distinguishes feature work (→ develop) from hotfixes (→ main). The same repo needs both at once. Per-task is the smallest unit that expresses this without inventing a "branch type" concept or a parallel "hotfix mode" lifecycle.

BC: the field is optional. Old bl reads the task JSON via lenient serde (no deny_unknown_fields on Task — confirmed in src/task.rs:114) and silently ignores the new field; its bl review will use the repo-level default or current branch. This is the accepted caveat: a hotfix task created with per-task override pointing at main, processed by an old client with develop checked out, will land on develop. Documented in the spec; do not engineer to prevent it.

ACCEPTANCE:
- bl create --target-branch main: writes target_branch field on the task.
- bl review on a task with target_branch set: uses that branch, ignoring both the repo-level target_branch and the current-branch fallback.
- bl show surfaces target_branch when non-null.
- Old-client decode of a Task with target_branch set: succeeds, field silently dropped.

Priority p3: nice-to-have, ship after the core deferred-squash work lands. Single-target-per-repo covers the most common case.
title: Per-task target_branch override
